message.content:
Explore the existing UI patterns in the iskrib_v3 codebase to understand design conventions for the paragraph commenting feature. I need:

1. The global CSS variables/theme from `C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\index.css` - especially dark mode variables under `[data-theme="dark"]`
2. The existing comment card patterns - look for any comment components in `C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\components/` (search for "comment" in component names/files)
3. How the existing Lexical editor renders paragraphs - check the editor theme classes (editor-paragraph) and how content is rendered in read-only mode
4. The ChapterReader CSS at `C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\components\Stories\ChapterReader\ChapterReader.css`
5. Check for any existing slide-in panel or modal patterns in the codebase (search for "panel", "drawer", "slide" in CSS files)
6. Check what animation library is available - look for framer-motion usage patterns
7. Check the existing auth modal or any overlay/panel pattern for reference

Focus on understanding the glass morphism style, CSS variable usage, and animation patterns so the new comment UI matches the existing aesthetic.
